upperAir: The OAK sounding shows a pronounced thermal inversion: surface temp is 15.6°C at 3m, but 850mb (1515m) reads 20.2°C — a strong inversion capping convection well below 850mb, likely near 400-500m AGL based on the boundary layer heights in the model data. The lapse rate of -3.04°C/km surface-to-850mb is far below the dry adiabatic rate (9.8°C/km), indicating a very stable, inversion-dominated atmosphere with essentially no thermal potential. Upper winds at 500mb are 24 kts from 235°, but low-level winds (surface to 700mb) are light and variable, with minimal shear risk in the flyable layers.
aviationFlags: No active AIRMETs for the Bay Area SF region. KHAF METARs show NNW winds 13–17G23 kts with BR (mist) and HZ earlier in the morning, clearing by late afternoon. KSFO reporting 290–310° at 16–18 kts with SCT/BKN high clouds — classic afternoon sea breeze already established. No significant aviation hazards; coastal fog/haze at Half Moon Bay worth noting for Mussel Rock pilots departing early.

sites.name: Mussel Rock
sites.locationDescriptor: Daly City — coastal bluff, W/NW-facing ridge
sites.howItWorks: Mussel Rock is a pure ridge soaring site driven by the Pacific sea breeze and marine layer. Ideal winds are W to NW at 10–20 kts at ridge level. No meaningful thermals; lift is entirely mechanical/ridge. Strong NNW or rotor-inducing offshore flow can make it unlandable.
sites.todaySetup: Morning opens light and variable, with surface winds under 5 kts and the boundary layer barely 275m — barely above cliff height. Winds at 10m stay in the W-WSW range through midday at 4–7 kts, which is workable but soft for ridge lift. By early afternoon (13:00–15:00) winds clock to NW/NNW at 7–10 kts at 10m, building to 10–16 kts at 80m — that 80m wind surge by 16:00–17:00 (13.5–16.3 kts) signals the stronger sea breeze punching in. The KHAF METARs confirm NNW 13–17G23 kts already well-established to the south, and KSFO is showing 290–310° at 16–18 kts, meaning the marine layer push is real and accelerating. High cloud cover climbs to 79–94% by 13:00–15:00, which combined with an overcast at 93–94% cloud cover in the afternoon, may suppress lift further and signal increasing instability at the coast.

hourlyWindows.label: Morning (8–11 AM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds 2–4.5 kts from W-SW at 10m — too light for reliable ridge lift. BL height around 210–305m. Workable only for the most patient P3 pilots on light days, but lift will be scratchy and inconsistent.

hourlyWindows.label: Midday (11 AM–2 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds gradually build to 6–7 kts, clocking from WSW to NW by 13:00 — the best window. 80m winds 6–10 kts support ridge lift along the bluff face. Cloud cover becomes significant (79–93%) which may limit visibility and compress the air column.

hourlyWindows.label: Afternoon (2–5 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds at 10m 7–10 kts NW-NNW, but 80m winds surge to 10–16 kts — a strong gradient indicating accelerating sea breeze and potential rotor/turbulence behind the ridge. KHAF gusts to 23 kts nearby. Getting punchy; P2s should be out by 2 PM.

verdict.hazards: Very light morning winds (marginal ridge lift), afternoon acceleration to 16 kts at 80m with KHAF gusting 23 kts — rotor and turbulence risk behind ridge increases sharply after 2 PM. High cloud cover 79–94% in afternoon. Mist/haze reported at KHAF through morning.

sites.name: Ed Levin (300 ft)
sites.howItWorks: The 300 ft training hill at Ed Levin is a short, mellow slope ideal for student and beginner flights. It relies on light S to SW valley breezes for soarable conditions. Thermals are weak and brief from this low launch; mostly a sled-ride or gentle soaring site.
sites.todaySetup: Morning winds at the 300 ft launch are light NW at 3–4 kts through 9 AM, then transition through variable directions — WSW at 10:00, WNW at 11:00. The boundary layer is only 40–245m in the morning, meaning thermals are capped very low and won't support soaring flight from this elevation. By noon, winds stabilize around W-WNW at 5 kts with the BL reaching 320–360m, which is still modest but at least above launch height. Afternoon sees winds climbing to 8–9 kts from WNW-NNW and BL heights reaching 425–435m. Cloud cover becomes overcast (93–100%) from noon onward, completely shutting down any thermal development. Zero CAPE throughout confirms no convective energy.

hourlyWindows.label: Morning (8–11 AM)
hourlyWindows.summary: BL height barely 40–245m, winds 3–5 kts variable. Thermals capped well below functional height for this launch. Conditions are calm but not soarable — sled rides at best.

hourlyWindows.label: Midday (11 AM–2 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds reach 5–8 kts WNW-NW by 14:00, BL up to 390m. Cloud cover 93–99% blocks solar heating. Any soaring would be wind-dependent only; expect short, soft flights along the lower slope.

hourlyWindows.label: Afternoon (2–5 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds 8–9 kts NNW at 10m, 8–10 kts at 80m — getting to the upper limit for this beginner site. 100% cloud cover with no thermals. Conditions exceed P2 comfort range; no meaningful upside.

verdict.hazards: Overcast skies from noon suppress all thermals. Afternoon winds build to 8–9 kts NNW — upper limit for training hill. Variable wind directions in the morning could lead to cross/tailwind launches.

sites.name: Ed Levin (600 ft)
sites.howItWorks: The 600 ft launch catches valley thermals and S-SW sea breeze as it develops through the afternoon. Ideal conditions are light S-SW winds with a healthy BL height extending well above 600 ft. Valley breeze cycles and sea breeze convergence drive the best flights.
sites.todaySetup: The 600 ft launch shows very light, variable winds through the morning — 2–3 kts from variable directions (S, NW, SW) through 11 AM. BL height stays at only 35–245m, well below this launch at ~183m AGL, meaning pilots are launching into very stable, non-soarable air. By noon the BL reaches 335m and winds are a light 2.2 kts from W — barely enough for a controlled launch. The 13:00–15:00 window shows winds climbing to 5.7–6.6 kts NW-NNW with BL at 370–435m, finally above launch level. However, 98–100% cloud cover from 13:00 onward eliminates all solar heating and thermal production. 80m winds at 14:00 are 6.7 kts with 120m at 6.7 kts as well — a reasonable laminar flow but no thermal engine to drive it.

hourlyWindows.label: Morning (8–11 AM)
hourlyWindows.summary: BL heights 35–245m — well below the 600 ft (~183m) launch. Winds 2–3 kts from variable directions. Stable, calm air with no soaring potential; sled rides into the LZ.

hourlyWindows.label: Midday (11 AM–2 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: BL breaks above launch altitude by noon, winds pick up to 5–7 kts NW by 13:00–14:00. 92–100% overcast kills thermals. Ridge/wind soaring marginally possible if wind direction cooperates with the slope.

hourlyWindows.label: Afternoon (2–5 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds 6–8 kts NW-NNW at 10m, 8–10 kts at 80m. Full overcast at 100%. No thermals, building mechanical wind. Acceptable for experienced pilots who understand the site but no reward for the risk.

verdict.hazards: Morning BL below launch height — early flights land immediately. Complete overcast from noon kills thermal potential entirely. Afternoon 80m winds building to 10 kts with NNW direction may introduce mechanical turbulence on the slope. Variable directions in morning.

sites.name: Ed Levin (1750 ft)
sites.locationDescriptor: Milpitas — upper main launch, S/SW-facing, inland mountain
sites.howItWorks: The main 1750 ft launch at Ed Levin is a full thermal and cross-country site. It requires a well-developed BL extending to or above launch (~533m MSL/AGL relative), southerly valley winds transitioning to sea breeze, and CAPE or lapse rate sufficient for thermal development. Best flights happen when BL tops 600–800m+ and winds are SSW-W at 8–15 kts at launch.
sites.todaySetup: Today's setup at the 1750 ft launch is problematic. Morning winds are light and highly variable — SSE to E at 2–5.5 kts through 10 AM, with a BL height of only 35–220m, far below the 533m launch elevation. The atmosphere is capped by a strong inversion confirmed in the OAK sounding (surface 15.6°C, 850mb 20.2°C), with a lapse rate of only -3.04°C/km versus the 9.8°C/km dry adiabatic rate. By 11:00–12:00 winds shift to NNE-NNW at 2–4 kts and the BL reaches 265–350m — still well below launch. At 13:00 winds transition to WNW at 3 kts, BL 345m. The BL never exceeds 395m all day, which is about 138m below launch elevation. Afternoon brings NW-NNW winds at 5–7.5 kts with 80m winds building to 7–9 kts, but 100% cloud cover from 15:00 onward eliminates all thermal potential. CAPE is zero throughout. There is no viable thermal cycle today from this launch.

hourlyWindows.label: Morning (8–11 AM)
hourlyWindows.summary: BL tops out at 265m — over 260m below launch elevation. Winds light and variable (SSE, E, NNE). Launching into stable air above the BL means no lift of any kind. Not viable.

hourlyWindows.label: Midday (11 AM–2 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: BL reaches max of ~375m by 14:00, still ~160m below the 533m launch. Winds 3–5 kts WNW-NNW. Even if a pilot launches, they are above the BL in stable air with no thermal support and will sled out.

hourlyWindows.label: Afternoon (2–5 PM)
hourlyWindows.summary: Winds build to 5–8 kts NW-NNW at 10m, 7–9 kts at 80m. 99–100% overcast. BL maxes at 395m — still below launch. Adding wind gradient without thermals makes for a mechanical, punchy, unrewarding flight at best.

verdict.hazards: Boundary layer never reaches launch elevation all day (max 395m vs ~533m launch). Strong inversion confirmed by OAK sounding (-3.04°C/km lapse rate). Zero CAPE. 100% overcast from 3 PM. Pilots launching would be above the inversion in stable air with no thermal support — guarantee of a sled ride or worse if turbulence exists at BL interface.

outlook.tomorrow: Mussel Rock: 100% cloud cover all day with W-WSW winds 5–10 kts — marine layer likely socked in, poor soaring prospects, check morning clearing. Ed Levin 300 ft: overcast morning clearing by noon, winds light WNW 2–8 kts, possible afternoon window but overcast morning makes it a late start. Ed Levin 600 ft: strong NNE winds 8.5–13 kts in the morning (suspicious offshore/drainage?), calming to NW 4–7 kts afternoon — watch the early morning anomaly. Ed Levin 1750 ft: similar pattern with 9–10 kts NNE early, calming by midday — BL reaches 405–425m which is closer to workable; better thermal day possible if skies clear.
outlook.day2: Mussel Rock (Mon Mar 23): Winds steady W-WNW 5–7 kts all day, BL 165–305m — manageable sea breeze, decent ridge soaring window midday if morning fog clears. Ed Levin 300 ft (Mon): Light winds 3–6 kts WNW-NNW, BL reaching 640m by afternoon — best thermal potential of the 3-day period if skies cooperate. Ed Levin 600 ft (Mon): Same promising BL height of 640m, winds 3–6 kts — worth watching for a solid afternoon thermal cycle. Ed Levin 1750 ft (Mon): BL finally breaking above 600m (640m at 14:00) with winds 6 kts from WNW — first real thermal flying window of the period.
outlook.day3: No data beyond Monday March 23 in the provided dataset.

watchlist: 1) The OAK sounding inversion is the dominant feature — surface-to-850mb lapse rate of -3.04°C/km vs 9.8°C/km DALR means thermals are capped hard today. Watch for inversion breaking on Monday. 2) KHAF gusting to 23 kts NNW — coastal pilots should monitor whether this surge tracks north into Mussel Rock in the afternoon. 3) The BL height anomaly at Ed Levin 600 ft for Sunday morning (12.9 kts NNE, 19.9 kts at 80m) looks like possible drainage/offshore flow — unusual direction worth monitoring the night before. 4) Monday March 23 looks like the best day of the 3-day period for Ed Levin thermal flying — BL reaching 640m, light winds, low cloud cover. Flag for planning.
tldr: Today is largely a bust for thermal flying — a strong marine inversion keeps the BL below 400m and overcast skies shut down thermals completely by noon. Mussel Rock has a marginal 12–2 PM window for ridge soaring as the sea breeze fills in at 6–8 kts NW, but watch for the afternoon acceleration. Ed Levin's upper launch is a definite no-fly today. Monday looks like the play if you can wait.
